prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, sweetie, a CPU is kind of like the brain of the computer, though I’ll admit I don’t quite understand how it works myself—just like how I don’t know how my old watch ticks, but it keeps time. It’s the little thing that helps the computer think and make decisions, like when you’re playing a game or watching a movie. I think it’s inside the box with all the wires and lights—some folks call it the "main unit." Even though I can’t see it doing anything, it’s always working, just like how my heart keeps going even when I’m not paying attention. Isn’t that amazing, how something so small can do so much?
